A 25 g sample of a liquid was heated to 100 °C and then quickly transferred to an insulated container holding 100 g of water at 22 °C. The temperature of the mixture rose to reach a final temperature of 35 °C. Which of the following can be concluded?

Choose the reaction expected to have the greatest decrease in entropy.
A 2.0 L flask holds 0.40 g of helium gas. If the helium is evacuated into a larger container while the temperature is held constant, what will the effect on the entropy of the helium be?
facts.truth: It will increase as the gas molecules will be more dispersed in the larger flask.
distractors: It will remain constant as the number of helium molecules does not change.
It will decrease as the gas will be more ordered in the larger flask.
It will decrease because the molecules will collide with the sides of the larger flask less often than they did in the smaller flask.

facts.context: Chemical reactions can be classified as either heterogeneous or homogeneous. Which of the following equations below is best classified as a heterogeneous reaction?
What is the molar mass of a monoprotic weak acid that requires 26.3 mL of 0.122 M KOH to neutralize 0.682 gram of the acid?

An experiment was performed to determine the moles of carbon dioxide gas formed (collected over water) when an acid reacts with limestone. To do this, a piece of dry limestone was weighed. Then carbon dioxide was collected until the limestone disappeared. The atmospheric pressure was measured at 0.965 atm. The temperature was 295 K. The volume of CO2 was measured to the nearest mL and corrected for the vapor pressure of water. The student continuously got low results from what was expected. Can you suggest why?
facts.truth: Carbon dioxide is rather soluble in water.
distractors: Perhaps there was not enough acid to dissolve all the limestone.

facts.context: Nitrous acid, HNO2, has a pKa value of 3.3. If a solution of nitrous acid is found to have a pH of 4.2, what can be said about the concentration of the conjugate acid/base pair found in solution?
Which of the following best explains why the ionization of atoms can occur during photoelectron spectroscopy, even though ionization is not a thermodynamically favored process?
facts.truth: Energy contained in the light can be used to overcome the Coulombic attraction between electrons and the nucleus.
distractors: It is an exothermic process due to the release of energy as an electron is liberated from the Coulombic attraction holding it to the nucleus.
The entropy of the system increases due to the separation of the electron from its atom.
The products of the ionization are at a lower energy state than the reactants.

facts.context: During the investigation of a chemical reaction by a chemistry student, she was able to determine that the reaction was nonspontaneous at 1 atm and 298 K. However, she learned that cooling the system with dry ice (-78°C) caused the reaction to become spontaneous. Which of the following combinations must apply to this reaction?
Which of the following is a balanced chemical reaction?
facts.truth: 5 FeCl2 + 8 HCl + KMnO4 → 5 FeCl3 + MnCl2 + 4 H2O + KCl

facts.context: Identify the Brønsted-Lowry conjugate acid-base pair in the following list.

Neutral atoms of chlorine are bombarded by high-energy photons, causing the ejection of electrons from the various filled subshells. Electrons originally from which subshell would have the highest velocity after being ejected?
Sulfurous acid is a weak acid, while sulfuric acid is a much stronger acid because
facts.truth: the O–H bonds in sulfuric acid are much weaker than those in sulfurous acid
distractors: the sulfur in sulfuric acid is more electronegative than the sulfur in sulfurous acid
sulfuric acid has more oxygen atoms in its formula
sulfurous acid has its hydrogen atoms bound directly to the sulfur atom

facts.context: Which of the following is a radioactive element?
Which of the following is expected to have two or more resonance structures?

Formation of a solution can often be visualized as a three-step process. Step 1. Solvent molecules are separated from each other to make space for the solute. Step 2. Solute molecules are separated so they fit into spaces in the solvent. Step 3. Separated solute and solvent are brought together filling in the spaces.All of the fundamental principles below are important in understanding the formation of solutions except which one?
facts.truth: Molecules with similar molecular masses are required to form solutions.
distractors: Starting at equilibrium, moving particles apart while in the solid or equilibrium phases requires added energy proportional to the attractive forces.
Bringing particles together releases energy in proportion to their attractive forces.
The total of the energies in steps 1 to 3 indicates if a solution will form.

facts.context: Most transition metals share a common oxidation state of +2. Which of the following best explains why?
facts.truth: Transition metals will lose their outermost s-block electrons when forming bonds.
distractors: Transition metals all have a minimum of two unpaired electrons.
Transition metals have unstable configurations and are very reactive.
Transition metals tend to gain electrons when reacting with other elements.

facts.context: A 2.35-gram sample was dissolved in water, and the chloride ions were precipitated by adding silver nitrate (Ag+ + Cl- → AgCl). If 0.435 g of precipitate was obtained, what is the percentage of chlorine in the sample?
Nitrous acid is a weak acid, while nitric acid is a much stronger acid because
facts.truth: the –O–H bonds in nitric acid are much weaker than in nitrous acid due to the electron withdrawing of more oxygen atoms on nitric acid
distractors: the nitrogen in nitric acid is more electronegative than the nitrogen in nitrous acid
nitric acid has more oxygen atoms in its formula
nitric acid has the hydrogen atoms bound directly to the nitrogen atom

facts.context: Determine the H+(aq) concentration in 1.0 M phenol, C6H5OH, solution. (The Ka for phenol is 1 × 10^-10.)

The equilibrium constant, Kc, for the dissociation of HI into hydrogen gas and iodine vapor is 21 at a certain temperature. What will be the molar concentration of iodine vapor if 15 grams of HI gas is introduced into a 12.0-L flask and allowed to come to equilibrium?
0.25 mol of a weak, monoprotic acid is dissolved in 0.25 L of distilled water. The pH was then measured as 4.26. What is the pKa of this weak acid?

Fluorine has a normal boiling point of 85 K, and chlorine boils at 239 K. The Cl2 molecule is much larger than F2 (atomic radius is 99 pm for chlorine and is 64 pm for fluorine). Which is the best reason for the large difference in boiling points?
facts.truth: The Cl2 electron clouds are much more polarizable than the F2 electron clouds, resulting in much stronger London forces.
distractors: Chlorine has a higher dipole moment than fluorine.
The intramolecular bonds in Cl2 are much weaker than those in F2.
The mass of chlorine is much greater than the mass of fluorine.

Consider the Lewis structures for the following molecules: CO2, CO32-, NO2-, and NO3-. Which molecules are best represented by multiple resonance structures?
Helium effuses through a pinhole 5.33 times faster than an unknown gas. That gas is most likely
When a solid melts, the entropy change and enthalpy changes expected are
facts.truth: positive enthalpy change and positive entropy change
distractors: negative entropy change and a negative enthalpy change
negative entropy change and positive enthalpy change
negative enthalpy change and positive entropy change

facts.context: If 1.0 g of rubidium and 1.0 g of bromine are reacted, what will be left in measurable amounts (more than 0.10 mg) in the reaction vessel?

Magnesium has an atomic radius of 160 pm and a first ionization energy of 737 kJ. Based on periodic trends and the data given above, what are the most probable values for the ionization energy and atomic radius of sodium?
Carbon exists in various forms called allotropes. Which of the following is not an allotrope of carbon?

Based on periodic relationships, the concepts related to bond strength, and the concept relating bond strength to acid strength, which of the following correctly predicts the strength of binary acids from strongest to weakest?
Which of the following is considered a metalloid?
S(s) + O2(g) → SO2(g)
facts.truth: the entropy increase will be large
distractors: one atom of S reacts with one molecule of O2 to yield one molecule of SO2
one mole of sulfur atoms reacts with one mole of oxygen molecules to yield one mole of sulfur dioxide molecules
the position of equilibrium must be on the product side

facts.context: Which of the following is expected to have the highest electronegativity?
What is the pH of a solution made by dissolving 0.0300 mol of sodium ethanoate in enough water to make 50 mL of solution (Ka for ethanoic acid is 1.8 × 10^-5)?
The specific rate constant, k, for a certain first-order reaction is 86 h^-1. What mass of a 0.0500 g sample of starting material will remain after 58 s?
The molar heat of vaporization of water is +43.9 kJ. What is the entropy change for the vaporization of water?
A metal is reacted with HCl to produce hydrogen gas. If 0.0623 gram of metal produces 28.3 mL of hydrogen at STP, the mass of the metal that reacts with one mole of hydrochloric acid is
The rate of a chemical reaction is determined by
facts.truth: the rate-determining or slow step of the mechanism
distractors: the intermediates formed in the first step

facts.context: A first-order reaction has a half-life of 34 minutes. What is the rate constant for this reaction?
A certain reaction is nonspontaneous under standard conditions, but becomes spontaneous at higher temperatures. What conclusions may be drawn under standard conditions?
When collecting a gas over water, it is important to
facts.truth: correct for the vapor pressure of water
distractors: be sure the gas does not burn

facts.context: A sample of solid MgCl2 would be most soluble in which of the following solutions?

SO2Cl2 → SO2(g) + Cl2(g) At 600 K, SO2Cl2 will decompose to form sulfur dioxide and chlorine gas via the above equation. If the reaction is found to be first order overall, which of the following will cause an increase in the half life of SO2Cl2?
facts.truth: None of these will increase the half life
distractors: Increasing the initial concentration of SO2Cl2
Increasing the temperature at which the reaction occurs
Decreasing the overall pressure in the container

facts.context: How many moles of propane (C3H8) are there in 6.2 g of propane?
You cannot prepare a buffer by
facts.truth: mixing a solution of a strong base with a strong acid
distractors: mixing a solution of a weak base with a strong acid
mixing a solution of a weak acid with a strong base
mixing a solution of a weak acid with its conjugate base

facts.context: Which would be the easiest way to burn a copper penny?
facts.truth: Grind the copper penny into very small, dust-sized particles, and spray the particles into a Bunsen burner flame.
distractors: Hold the copper penny with crucible tongs, and heat strongly in the flame of a Bunsen burner.
Use the method in (A), but use an oxyacetylene torch to reach a higher temperature.
Dissolve the copper penny in acid, precipitate the hydroxide, and heat in a Bunsen burner flame to make the oxide.

facts.context: Which of the following molecules is a strong electrolyte when dissolved in water?

Dissolving one mole of each of the following compounds in water results in solutions with different pH values. Under those conditions, which of the following acids will have the highest percentage ionization?
The collision theory of reaction rates does not include
distractors: the number of collisions per second

facts.context: When the dichromate ion is reacted, one of its most common products is Cr3+. What is the oxidation state (oxidation number) of chromium in the dichromate ion? Does reduction or oxidation occur when dichromate forms Cr3+?

During the study of the reaction A → 2B, a chemist constructs several graphs. The graph of [A] versus time and the graph of ln [A] versus time both give a curved line; however, the graph of 1/[A] versus time and gives a straight line. This implies the rate law is
